title: Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw on Innovation, GLP-1 Race & ...
text:
Let's get chatting with Kiran Majumar
Shaw who's the executive chairperson of
BCON. Kiran, always a pleasure to
welcome you on NDTV Profit. Now, as we
wind down on this particular year,
Kiran, this has been a remarkable year
with many many milestones that have been
achieved which have been in the works
for many years for uh you personally and
your ambition in the company Bioon. So
first of all I would like to ask you
with this merger biologics bioon as a
combined force what it means for you in
the journey for the company.
>> So thanks Nisha and let me start by
wishing you a very very happy 2026
ahead.
>> Thank you. the same to you.
>> And let me start by saying yes, this has
been a very very eventful year for us
>> and I think the recent announcement that
we made of uh the intended merger
between BBL and Bioon is indeed a big
announcement because I believe that u
this actually unlocks and creates the
value that we have been uh you know
witnessing in terms of our bioimilars
business and as well as the GLP1
opportunity ahead. And we built we felt
that by combining the two businesses
actually we would unlock a lot of
shareholder value uh for uh Bioon as a a
conglomerate. And we believe that this
creates a bigger company, a much uh more
diversified entity and a simpler and
elegant model which actually then helps
us to focus on uh you know really
leveraging the opportunity that we have
of being uniquely positioned to combine
both insulins and GLP1s uh you know the
insulin bioimilars and the generic
GLP1s.
So I think this is a big uh you know
opportunity for us now to drive big
value and I think this benefits all
stakeholders. That's
>> right and it's a big big development and
next from next year onwards the
stakeholders shareholders in the company
will start seeing the benefit of the
combined force and Kiran I know for sure
that it has been in the works for
several years putting all the pieces
together. Now before we go to much
larger conversation and big picture
outlook for 2026, I wanted to ask you
about one news development which is
about securing the global manufacturing
and commercial rights for Julio. What
does this mean and how will it benefit
the company?
Well, you know, uh, Julio was an asset
where we had a basically a a uh,
transfer pricing, profit sharing
arrangement, etc. And, uh, I think once
we basically got the whole business uh,
you know, as as we have just discussed,
we believed in the that it was important
for the biosimilars business to be
actually capturing the entire value. So
I think from that point of view uh we
believe this is a big deal and you know
it now brings the asset under our full
control. We believe we can get higher
margins by doing this and we also
believe that we will be able to unlock
significant uh growth opportunities uh
having this uh advantage in terms of
both cost efficiency and uh you know the
kind of opportunities that we have ahead
in terms of Adelimu
>> right uh so this is also going to uh
it's a much larger play uh in what you
had a commercial tie up with. So that's
really extending the benefits that could
come out of it. But Kiran, as you look
back on 2025, of course, many milestones
for the company itself, but as a leader,
industry leader in the healthcare space,
how do you see this particular year for
the entire sector and what were the key
highlights that came across for you?
So Nisha to me the biggest highlight was
the announcement of ANRF.
I think this has been a very important
step taken by the government to
basically focus on IPdriven economic
growth through research and innovation
and I think this is really going to help
us all big time in every sector
especially even in the pharmaceutical
sector. Added to this of course is the
RDI which of course helps the pharma
sector particularly uh because I think a
lot of companies are beginning to invest
in research and innovation that will
drive even greater value. As you know up
until now we've been really focusing on
bioimilars and generics which of course
are very important growth drivers for
the pharma sector. But going ahead, can
we actually focus on innovative drugs
and create even greater value is the
opportunity we are looking at. In
addition to that, I think we are also
seeing the emergence of uh you know
affordable innovation across the world
and I think India can lead in that even
in cell and gene therapy which is such a
big challenge for the world to access. I
think India has taken the lead in
delivering affordable access to even you
know carti therapies and gene therapies
hopefully in the future. I think there's
also a very strong focus on ADCs and RNA
technologies and synthetic biology. So a
very very interesting 2025
where companies have really focused on
these emerging areas. ANRF is going to
focus on critical technologies and I
think this is going to augur well for
the entire sector and especially for
companies like Bioon that has always
believed in in uh you know a
researchdriven uh approach and an
innovationdriven approach to its
business.
>> That's right. In fact, Kiran, it's been
several years that we have been chatting
about how India can make that shift from
generic leadership to innovation
leadership. For the next year, that is a
much shorter time period. How do you see
this developing and the key aspects that
you're watching out for in 2026?
>> So, apart from what I've just mentioned,
you know, Nisha, because you know that
it's going to take time for innovation
to really deliver. So as long as we
start that journey, I think there's a
big opportunity ahead. But I think what
is even more important is for us to
notice what has happened at the macro
level. One is of course the biosecure
act which is going to really really open
up a bigger opportunity in CRDMO
and this is something which India must
double down on which it already has in
many ways but it must get a much more
significant market share and I think
here the whole focus on biio
manufacturing is going to help us a lot.
The bio3 policy focuses on biio
manufacturing and I think whilst bioon
has been the front leader in this uh I
think other companies also ought to then
now focus on uh investing in biio
manufacturing at global scale. um as you
know and this of course opens up a much
bigger opportunity for India as a whole
and then as I mentioned affordability is
the theme for the global pharma uh
ecosystem and and you know that uh
President Trump himself is very focused
on uh affordable access to uh you know
drugs and pharmaceuticals in general and
I think this is also playing to our
strength because I think here the MFN uh
condition that he's lev to most of the
big pharma is actually an advantage to
us and I think we are now going to stop
seeing cross subsidizing of of
pharmaceutical products in various
markets and this actually then opens up
an even bigger opportunity for companies
like uh bioon
>> right uh so Kiran I know that uh how
well connected you are in the global
scientific uh world when it comes to
healthcare space and all those points
that you mentioned I'm sure is something
that is the view globally that is really
emerging but when you look at bioon
going forward how is the positioning of
bioon that you would like to see in the
world of course your push towards
innovation in the last few years has
also been very very critical
>> so you know Nisha first and foremost I'm
very proud of the fact that bioon has
already attained leadership in
bioimilars amongst the top five
biosimilar companies globally and the
only one out of India to really gain
that kind of recognition. We have a very
strong pipeline of products. We are also
going to be the only company with
bioimilar interchangeable insulins in
com combination with generic uh you know
GLP1s which again positions us very
uniquely. In addition to that we have a
very large and growing pipeline of
biosimilars and specialty generics as
well. Uh we are very strong apart from
the diabetesity space as we call it in
oncology and immunology. Again bioon's
generic business plays to this strength
because we have very strong generic
oncology products and we have a very
strong presence in imunosuppressants
which adds to the immunology aspect. So
it's going to be a very exciting year
ahead and of course time ahead for bioon
by bioon as a company as it merges into
a single entity and we will have a very
simple elegant structure of you know
products and services uh services
through singing and of course products
through the bioimilars and generics
which really makes us a very unique
Indian company with this kind of spread
of products. So I I'm very excited with
what is happening to our business and
how we've shaped our business and the
opportunities ahead.
>> You mentioned GLP now with Lira Tide
also uh launched in Europe as well as
India. How's Bioon positioned in the
global GLP1 race? Um Kiran
>> obviously everyone's you know eyeing
this very lucrative pie where you know
semiglutide is going to go off patent in
many markets including Canada in
addition to that of course uh you know
bioon we believe have got a a
competitive advantage because of our uh
global scale in in in the insulins
business because GLP1s is not just about
a you know a product play like any other
product. It is about a device play. It
is about a uh at scale drug product and
drug substance capability. And you just
heard that you know uh the uh oral GLP1
of semiglutide which is the ribbelis has
just got approved in the US and this
actually needs recominant DNA capacity
to make it at such large scale which
bioon has. So I think this puts us into
a very strong situation because we will
have endto-end capabilities when it
comes to GLP1s. Of course it's a very
funible uh space when it comes to
insulins because we've already created
global scale manufacturing whether it's
drug substance or drug products and you
know peptides whether they're insulins
or GLP1s are very funible. So I think
it's it's it's going to you know give us
uh an added advantage and that's why we
believe we are uniquely placed.
>> So you're uniquely placed for GLP1 but
how meaningful will this be in a near
term of say 3 to 5 years Kiran as
compared to bioimilars your GLP1 uh
push? Well, you know, GLP1s is a big
opportunity. It's a huge opportunity
because, you know, you haven't even
sized the whole uh market as yet for the
simple reason that uh GLP1's right now
is is is has not been fully explored and
uh leveraged in emerging markets because
you know the the GLP1 companies were
really focused on the advanced markets.
So this is going to add to the growing
uh you know opportunity of JLP1s and I
think companies are all focused on that.
Of course the real opportunities and
advanced markets open up only in u in in
u you know 2030 onwards when semiglutide
goes off patent but in the interim of
course there's a very large opportunity
in emerging markets especially Canada
everyone's eyeing that that that
opportunity. Uh, of course, Bioon wants
to play a good role and a lead a frontr
running role in that. Yes.
>> And we believe that because of what I
just mentioned, we do have that
opportunity to play a very significant
role.
>> But I must say that this is going to be
a big opportunity for everyone who wants
to play in it. But they've got to have
global scale to be competitive, which we
believe we have at Bioon.
>> All right. So, you have that global
scale. Before we let you go Kiran, a few
of house housekeeping questions about
bioon after the 4,500 cr rupees of QIP
uh do you think now this u uh you know
equity dilution for now is sufficient
how long is it going to last and what's
your comfortable debt a bit levels that
you are looking at post the integration
>> so Nisha first and foremost you know we
are having this QIP really to address
the the part cash part stock deal that
we have uh entered into with biatric and
once we are you know once we are post
that I think we are in a very healthy
position because as a company we will be
you know below the 2.5 uh debt to eida
ratio and we believe going ahead it'll
even go below two because of the kind of
business that is growing and the kind of
eidas that we're generating so
organically itself we'll be able to
reduce the uh the eid debt eida ratio to
a very comfortable level and I think
going forward you will see this business
growing stronger and stronger and
combining the businesses has actually
helped us to deliver a very strong
balance sheet so I just think that we've
done everything that needs to be done to
put this business back on a very sound
track on in a very healthy way on a very
strong finan financial footing.
>> So Kiran, you know, your excitement
about the next year about the business
of Bioon is really infectious and uh
that's really great to hear as we wind
down on 2025. Thank you so much for
joining us right here on NDTV Profit.
Have a great new year and we will
connect with you in 2026. Thank you so
much Kiran.
>> Thank you Nisha.
>> [music]
